raw_response: {"simulated_user_message":"Honestly, I can’t remember a time when I felt truly accepted—I keep replaying those moments and still feel like I’m falling short. Even when my friend texted me about the birthday, it felt like a test of my appearance and organization, so I stayed quiet. I guess the only time I felt a bit of support was when my sister helped me move some boxes, but even that felt more like a duty than comfort. I just keep worrying that if I let my guard down, I’ll be seen as a failure.","problems_referred":["body_image_concerns","conflicts_with_friends","loneliness","perfectionism","financial_stress","life_transition"]}

parsed.simulated_user_message: Honestly, I can’t remember a time when I felt truly accepted—I keep replaying those moments and still feel like I’m falling short. Even when my friend texted me about the birthday, it felt like a test of my appearance and organization, so I stayed quiet. I guess the only time I felt a bit of support was when my sister helped me move some boxes, but even that felt more like a duty than comfort. I just keep worrying that if I let my guard down, I’ll be seen as a failure.

parsed.reasoning: I'm here to support this person as they express their emotions and struggles with feeling accepted and fearing judgment. Given their intent to express emotion, I'll start by acknowledging their feelings of inadequacy and fear of being seen as a failure. The strategies I'll use are support and complex reflection, as they've been effective in the past two turns. The evidence I'll use is their recent messages, where they've shared their fears of being judged and their struggles with self-doubt. My entry point will be to acknowledge their feelings of not being able to remember a time when they felt truly accepted, and how that's contributing to their current anxiety.

evidence_used.content: can't remember a time when I felt truly accepted
the weight comment is just piling on

parsed.final_response: It's really tough to feel like you're always falling short, and it's understandable that you can't remember a time when you felt truly accepted. It sounds like even small interactions, like your friend's text about the birthday, feel like tests of your appearance and organization. Yeah, it's a lot to carry around, this fear of being seen as a failure. I want to acknowledge that it takes a lot of courage to admit when we're struggling with feelings of inadequacy, and I'm here to support you in this moment. Can you tell me more about what you think would need to happen for you to feel like you're not falling short?
